task_context.task: navigate: Locate the most recently published blog post on Hugging Face concerning advancements in multimodal AI technology.
task_context.title: Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL

observation.title: Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL

args.query: run opens Hugging Face blog, searches for multimodal AI technology, then lands on a specific article about realistic image generation in OdysseyXL instead of the most recent relevant multimodal AI post; likely wrong target or missed search relevance

items.summary: The agent used broad browsing steps instead of applying the available search or filter controls.
items.fix_hint: Prefer task-specific search inputs and filters when the target can be narrowed directly.

items.summary: The agent operated on the wrong target — wrong entity, wrong location, wrong category, or wrong page type — instead of the user's intended object.
items.fix_hint: Verify that the active page or selected entity matches the user's stated target before acting; recognize when the current context does not match and reorient.

items.summary: The agent completed a search task but did not enforce an explicit user constraint before stopping.
items.fix_hint: Track hard constraints separately from the main goal and verify them against the final page state before returning success.

args.query: Hugging Face blog search lands on unrelated article or wrong target after search query about multimodal AI technology

args.task: navigate: Locate the most recently published blog post on Hugging Face concerning advancements in multimodal AI technology.

evidence.claim: The run reaches a Hugging Face blog article page titled “Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL” at step 6, showing a specific article was opened.

High-detail inspection of step 5 shows the autocomplete/result suggestion highlighted is “Advancements in Realistic Image Generation in OdysseyXL,” and the action is pressing Enter to activate that suggestion.

The same inspection notes that the intended constraint “concerning advancements in multimodal AI technology” is not visibly supported by the suggestion/article text, and that “most recently published” is not visible from the opened page alone.
High-detail inspection of step 6 shows the opened article is a Community Article published July 12, 2025, but no comparison evidence is visible to prove it is the most recent relevant post.
The run ends with a send-message “[EXIT]” action rather than any additional verification step on article recency or multimodal relevance.
Failure-memory context indicates similar mistakes include wrong target selection and missed constraints during navigation tasks.
Failure-memory context also indicates that stopping without verifying an explicit task constraint is a recognized missed-constraint pattern.

args.expected_behavior: After opening search results, verify that the chosen blog post actually matches the multimodal-AI topic and is the most recently published relevant post before exiting; if not evident, continue browsing/searching until the constraint is satisfied.
args.actual_behavior: The agent activated a suggestion that led to a specific article about realistic image generation, then exited without establishing that the article concerned multimodal AI or that it was the most recently published relevant post.
args.regression_rule: When a navigation task includes both topical relevance and recency, do not stop at a plausible-looking result; confirm both the topic match and the recency claim from visible evidence before ending the run.

suggested_followups.label: Inspect step 5
suggested_followups.message: Show the high-detail evidence for the Enter-on-suggestion step and why it may not satisfy the multimodal-AI constraint.

suggested_followups.label: Inspect step 6
suggested_followups.message: Open the article step in detail to check the publication date and visible article context.

suggested_followups.label: Compare successful run
suggested_followups.message: Find a validated successful run for a similar Hugging Face blog navigation task and compare the verification pattern.
